site: Sherman Mega-Site — SM1 fab (first of four 300mm wafer fabs)
sum: Texas Instruments began production at SM1, the first of four planned 300mm wafer fabs on its Sherman mega-site, on December 17, 2025 — three and a half years after breaking ground. The site is planned for up to four connected fabs with a combined $40B investment and up to 3,000 direct jobs. SM2's exterior shell is already complete.
cre: Anchor industrial occupancy confirmed; ~$40B multi-phase fab campus creates sustained demand for large-bay industrial, supplier, and workforce-housing product in Grayson County and the Texoma corridor.
changeNote: Production start confirmed Dec 17, 2025 (SM1 online); SM2 shell complete; previously only announced/under construction.

site: Samsung Austin Semiconductor — Taylor Foundry Campus
sum: Samsung confirmed limited fab operations began in February 2026 at its Taylor campus; the site is in final construction phases and targets full operations by end of 2026. The CHIPS Act award was renegotiated from $6.4B down to $4.745B as total capex scaled from $44B to $37B. The facility plans 2nm leading-edge logic production, supported by a Tesla chip-supply agreement. Employee transition from Austin campus began November 2025, targeting ~1,500 on-site workers by year-end 2026.
cre: Leading-edge foundry campus on 1,200 acres in Williamson County is pulling supplier and support-services tenants to Taylor, Hutto, and neighboring submarkets — strong near-term driver for industrial and flex product within a 30-minute radius.
changeNote: Limited operations confirmed Feb 2026; CHIPS award finalized at $4.745B (down from $6.4B); total capex scaled to $37B; Tesla anchor customer agreement disclosed.

site: Bastrop Advanced Chip Packaging & PCB Facility
sum: SpaceX began installing semiconductor packaging equipment at its Bastrop facility in April 2026, targeting RF chip production for Starlink by end of 2026. The expansion adds approximately 1 million sq ft to an existing ~1.1M sq ft footprint, potentially reaching 1.6M sq ft total. A $17.3M Texas Semiconductor Innovation Fund grant supports a dedicated semiconductor research and advanced packaging wing expected to create 400+ jobs. State officials describe it as the largest printed-circuit-board and panel-level packaging facility planned in North America.
cre: 1.6M-sq-ft industrial campus expanding in Bastrop County (~30 miles east of Austin) signals major occupancy and construction activity; associated Musk-entity land control (~700 acres in Bastrop County) and supplier pull create industrial and commercial leasing opportunity east of Austin.

site: John Palmour Manufacturing Center for Silicon Carbide
sum: Wolfspeed emerged from Chapter 11 bankruptcy on September 29, 2025, eliminating ~$4.6B in debt via a 91-day prepackaged restructuring. The Siler City John Palmour Center (2M sq ft, world's first high-volume 200mm SiC wafer fab) reached 'production readiness' in late fiscal 2025 but carries significant under-utilization costs. The $750M proposed CHIPS PMT has not been finalized; project status remains uncertain as of April 2026 per multiple trackers. Company is operationally restructured but financially fragile.
cre: 2M-sq-ft SiC wafer fab in Chatham County — one of the largest industrial buildings in North Carolina — is operational but running well below capacity; uncertain CHIPS funding and post-bankruptcy fragility create watch-and-wait posture for adjacent supplier and services development.
changeNote: Wolfspeed exited Chapter 11 bankruptcy Sept 29, 2025; Siler City fab reached production readiness late fiscal 2025 but is under-utilized; $750M CHIPS PMT unfinalized; project flagged as status-uncertain as of Apr 2026.
